results.summary: Selfish ambition (eritheia) is paired with jealousy as an inner disposition that produces disorder and every vile practice.

results.note: shever here describes external military/physical devastation of the land and tents, not an inner-life state.
mich.yah here refers to raw/living flesh in a skin lesion — purely physical diagnostic context, no inner-being content

results.summary: Titus is described as Paul's partner, sharing in a joint ministry relationship oriented toward serving others.
God's judgements are rules Israel must carefully observe throughout their life in the promised land.

results.note: God's seeing here is a divine evaluative act on external creation, not an inner-being window for human experience.
tunchanō used as 'perhaps' in travel planning context; no inner-life content
astheneō here refers to physical illness being healed, no inner-being content in view
H7503 here is a command to the angel to withdraw/stop physical action; no inner-life content conveyed through this term.
liver here is a physical organ removed in sacrificial ritual, no inner-being content

results.summary: Shimei expressed contempt toward David by cursing him as he walked, showing open scorn and hostility.

results.note: The term here describes physical mountains and hills; no inner-being content in this verse.

results.summary: Paul is entrusted with a stewardship (commission) whether he preaches willingly or not, showing God's trust placed in him.
results.note: Passive use: God has entrusted Paul with a gospel stewardship — relational trust between God and Paul.
chamal used as military command to show no mercy; no inner-life window opened here
H5715 appears as part of the superscription title 'Shushan Eduth' — a musical/liturgical label, not an inner-being use of testimony.
'hand' here is purely physical — people carried provisions in their hands; no inner-life content.
Here 'inheritance' refers to the land God gives Israel — a territorial/material gift, not an inner-being quality.
basar here means meat/food to eat — purely physical hunger, no inner-being window
'Name' here is simply a label identifying the rider as Death — no inner-life content conveyed through onoma.
prey here is physical food for lions; no inner-being content via this term
kalah here denotes the physical/temporal completion of writing; no inner-life content conveyed through this term
methermēneuō here simply explains the translation of a name; no inner-life content carried by this term.
ha.phakh here describes God overthrowing wicked rulers externally; no inner-life window through this term
peira here means 'experienced/underwent' mocking and flogging — describes external suffering, no inner-life window via this term
metatithēmi here describes physical transport of bodies to a tomb — purely a spatial/physical event with no inner-life content.
H8201 here refers to God's punitive acts against Egypt — external divine judgments, no inner-life window on the term itself.
dunatos here refers to military capability/capacity; inner deliberation is carried by G1011 bouleusetai (plan/deliberate)
tamam here means the scroll was entirely consumed by fire — a physical completion with no inner-life content.
H8535 tam (complete/innocence) does not appear in the spans; the verse describes a metallurgical/refining process with no inner-being window via this term.
H2491B not present in verse spans; H2491A appears as 'slain' (physical death), no inner-being content
H1823 here describes the visual appearance of supernatural creatures; no inner-life content.
Ritual uncleanness here applies to a physical vessel/object, not a person's inner life.
'tsar' here denotes external military/political enemies who devoured Israel; inner-being content (guilt, hope) is carried by other terms.

results.summary: God's way brings terror/destruction to evildoers, contrasting the security it gives the blameless.

results.note: Roaring describes the aggressive military advance of an enemy — external predatory action, no inner-life content.

results.summary: Evil distress will befall Israel in future days as consequence of their moral corruption and turning from God's commanded way.

results.note: Hearing here is physical/informational reception of news about a gathering — no inner-life window opened by this term in context.
Bones here are physical; youthful vigor dying with the person is bodily mortality, not inner-being content.
Inner-being worship content is carried by H7812 (bow/worship, R176); H1288 here means 'kneel' in physical posture context, not blessing with inner-life content.
sha.phel here describes physical destruction of fortified walls — no inner-life content.
basileuō here describes God's sovereign rule, not a human inner-being characteristic.
sheqer used as a dismissal of a reported message ('that is not true/falsehood'), no inner-life content engaged

results.summary: Simon pleads with the apostles to intercede for him, fearing the judgment spoken over him.

results.note: Eyes here refer to the man's literal physical eyes that Jesus healed; no inner-being content through this term.
hegeh here refers to the rumbling sound from God's mouth (thunder), not inner mourning or meditation
'foot' here is anatomical/locative reference only, no inner-being content carried by this term
skotizō here refers to the literal darkening/failing of the sun, a physical phenomenon with no inner-being content in this verse.
nes here is a physical signal/marker on a hill, used as a simile for Israel's remnant — no inner-life content carried by this term
ra.sha here labels the murderer's legal status deserving death; no inner-life window opened by this term in context.
The sound of the bugle here is a physical/auditory signal; the verse uses it as an analogy but the term itself carries no inner-being content.
shamar here means to guard/watch the house — a physical/custodial task with no inner-being content in this term's usage.

results.summary: God's character is entirely without injustice — he is perfectly faithful, righteous, and upright.

results.note: yad here means 'beside' (beside the way of the gate) — purely spatial/positional usage, no inner-being content.
H6381 here means 'to make a special/extraordinary vow' — a legal/ritual act, not an inner wonder or amazement.

results.summary: God has caused his people to eat and drink tears abundantly, expressing deep grief and suffering.

results.note: Sister here is a kinship identifier only; no inner-life content is expressed through this term in the verse.
H2942 here is a royal administrative decree about building specifications — no inner-life content.

results.summary: The servant's person (nephesh) is deeply despised and abhorred, yet God's faithfulness will vindicate them before kings.
results.note: nephesh here = the whole person who experiences contempt and rejection
H1921 here describes splendid appearance/garments — external magnificence, not inner-being content.
H3372G here describes a 'terrible land' — an external quality of a place, not an inner-being state.
Plain death/burial record; no inner-life content.
ta'ar here means 'sheath' (scabbard) for a sword — a physical object with no inner-being content.

results.summary: Haman was filled with rage against Mordecai because Mordecai refused to rise or show fear before him.
Paul challenges readers' lack of awareness about their baptism into Christ's death and its meaning for their inner life.

results.note: ta.her here refers to ritual/physical cleansing of the land by burying corpses, not inner-life purity.
H4339 here describes wine going down smoothly/evenly — physical sensation, not inner-being uprightness
H2865 here describes the ground/land as 'dismayed' (cracked/ruined by drought) — physical/agricultural condition, not inner-being state; farmers' shame is carried by H0954
H0539 here means 'nurse/foster-mother' — a caregiving role, not an inner-being quality of faithfulness.

results.summary: A worthless (Belial) counselor plots evil against the Lord, characterising inner moral corruption and malicious intent.

results.note: The rage/raging here refers to the storm sea ceasing, not a person's inner emotional state.

results.summary: Those who trusted and followed Judas the Galilean were scattered after his defeat, showing the outcome of misplaced allegiance.

results.note: The term H5327B (to desolate) does not appear in the verse spans; the actual strife term present is H5327A (to struggle), which describes physical conflict. Inner-being content is minimal; the verse is about legal consequences of physical harm.
Covenant here refers to the physical ark of the covenant being transported; no inner-life content engaged.
Sickness here refers to physical disease removed by God; no inner-life content via this term.
H3190 (be good) does not appear among the verse spans listed; verse concerns investigation of idolatry with no clear inner-being use of yatav here.
Length here refers to physical dimensions of the tabernacle court — no inner-being content.
Bronze here refers to physical chains/fetters binding Zedekiah — material object, no inner-being content.
kol (all) here is a quantifier describing the gathered crowd; no inner-being content is carried by this term.
rosh used as simile for corrupt judgment springing up like poisonous weeds — botanical/agricultural imagery, not inner-being content
H3665 here means 'subdue/bring low' applied to a defeated enemy nation — external military conquest, no inner humility content.
sōzō here refers to physical healing of a crippled man, not inner-being salvation
H6031B here means 'be daunted/humbled' by noise — describes lion's unperturbed response, not an inner-being characteristic in this simile context

results.summary: Failing to find her lovers, Israel resolves to return to her first husband, recognising her former state was better.
God is described as a righteous judge, whose righteousness means he rules with moral integrity and holds to just standards daily.

results.note: The term here refers to a choice/desirable vessel as a simile for the fallen leaders — material/object sense, no inner-life window.
lev here refers to the physical centre/chest of Absalom's body where javelins were thrust — no inner-life content.
H5462 here means 'pure/refined gold' (a nominal use meaning shut/enclosed gold), referring to a material, not an inner-being concept.
The term here refers to musical melody/sound on instruments, not inner meditation.

results.summary: The chief priests promise to keep the soldiers free from anxiety or trouble if the resurrection report reaches the governor.

results.note: qodesh here denotes the physical location (Holy Place) where priestly garments are used; no inner-being content carried by this term in this verse.
The scourge/whip is a physical instrument of punishment/consequence, not an inner-being term here.
Prophet here refers to the role/channel of divine speech; no inner-being content engaged.
The verse speaks of remembering a name and nations praising; yadah here describes external act of praise/thanksgiving without inner-life window in this verse.
peh here means 'opening/mouth of the sack' — a physical/spatial reference, no inner-being content

results.summary: Jerusalem's abominations exceed even Samaria's, making her sinful sisters look righteous by comparison.

results.note: qodesh here refers to consecrated offerings (ritual/institutional category), not an inner-life quality.
aitia here means 'the case/grounds' in a legal-relational sense about marriage conditions, not an inner-being quality.
ephah is a unit of dry measure for barley — purely quantitative/physical, no inner-being content
doulos here refers to servants sent on an errand; no inner-life content attaches to the term in this verse
flax here refers to linen fabric material, no inner-being content
